---
title: "Inventory Insights"
---

The tree inventory reveals patterns in tree health and distribution across Rittenhouse and Redd Parks.

## Tree Inventory Map 

```{python}
#| echo: false
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd

trees = pd.read_csv("data/form-1__tree-inventory.csv")

fig = go.Figure()

# Filter out rows with missing coordinates
trees_with_coords = trees.dropna(subset=['lat_6_Location', 'long_6_Location'])

# Color set
colors = (
    px.colors.qualitative.Plotly +
    px.colors.qualitative.Pastel
)

# Get unique park locations
parks = trees_with_coords["2_Park_Location"].unique().tolist()
parks_sorted = sorted([p for p in parks if pd.notna(p)])

for i, park in enumerate(parks_sorted):
    park_trees = trees_with_coords[trees_with_coords["2_Park_Location"] == park]
    
    if not park_trees.empty:
        fig.add_trace(
            go.Scattermap(
                lat=park_trees["lat_6_Location"],
                lon=park_trees["long_6_Location"],
                mode="markers",
                name=park,
                marker=dict(
                    size=8,
                    opacity=0.8,
                    color=colors[i % len(colors)]
                ),
                text=(park_trees["2_Park_Location"].astype(str) + "-" +
                      park_trees["3_Tree_ID"].astype(str)),
                customdata=park_trees[[
                    "5_Tree_Height_ft", "7_Diameter_at_Breast", "10_Tree_Condition"
                ]],
                hovertemplate=
                    "<b>%{text}</b><br>" +
                    "Height: %{customdata[0]} ft<br>" +
                    "DBH: %{customdata[1]}<br>" +
                    "Condition: %{customdata[2]}<br>" +
                    "<extra></extra>"
            )
        )

fig.update_layout(
    title=dict(
        text="Tree Inventory Map",
        x=0.5,
        xanchor='center',
        font=dict(size=15)
    ),
    map=dict(
        style="carto-positron",
        zoom=14,
        center=dict(
            lat=trees_with_coords["lat_6_Location"].mean(),
            lon=trees_with_coords["long_6_Location"].mean()
        )
    ),
    margin=dict(r=0, t=30, l=0, b=0),
    height=600
)

fig
```

<br/>
**Figure 1.** Tree inventory map showing locations of surveyed trees across parks.

## Tree Condition Summary

```{python}
#| echo: false
import plotly.express as px

trees_clean = trees[trees["10_Tree_Condition"].notna()]

if not trees_clean.empty:
    condition_counts = trees_clean["10_Tree_Condition"].value_counts()
    
    fig = px.bar(
        x=condition_counts.index,
        y=condition_counts.values,
        labels={'x': 'Tree Condition', 'y': 'Count'},
        title='Tree Condition Distribution',
        color=condition_counts.index,
        color_discrete_map={
            'Good': '#00CC96',
            'Fair': '#636EFA',
            'Poor': '#EF553B',
            'Stump': '#808080'
        }
    )
    
    fig.show()
```

<br/>
**Figure 2.** Distribution of tree conditions across the inventory.
